description:
cloudstack_instance

Creates and automatically starts a virtual machine based on a service offering, disk offering, and template.
example_usage:
resource "cloudstack_instance" "web" {
  name             = "server-1"
  service_offering = "small"
  network_id       = "6eb22f91-7454-4107-89f4-36afcdf33021"
  template         = "CentOS 6.5"
  zone             = "zone-1"
}

argument_reference.name: display_name
argument_reference.description: The display name of the instance.

argument_reference.name: service_offering
argument_reference.description: The name or ID of the service offering used for this instance.

argument_reference.name: network_id
argument_reference.description: The ID of the network to connect this instance to. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

argument_reference.name: ip_address
argument_reference.description: The IP address to assign to this instance. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

argument_reference.name: template
argument_reference.description: The name or ID of the template used for this instance. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

argument_reference.name: root_disk_size
argument_reference.description: The size of the root disk in gigabytes. The root disk is resized on deploy. Only applies to template-based deployments. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

argument_reference.name: group
argument_reference.description: The group name of the instance.

argument_reference.name: affinity_group_ids
argument_reference.description: List of affinity group IDs to apply to this instance.

argument_reference.name: affinity_group_names
argument_reference.description: List of affinity group names to apply to this instance.

argument_reference.name: security_group_ids
argument_reference.description: List of security group IDs to apply to this instance. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

argument_reference.name: security_group_names
argument_reference.description: List of security group names to apply to this instance. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

argument_reference.name: project
argument_reference.description: The name or ID of the project to deploy this instance to. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

argument_reference.name: zone
argument_reference.description: The name or ID of the zone where this instance will be created. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

argument_reference.name: start_vm
argument_reference.description: This determines if the instances is started after it is created (defaults true)

argument_reference.name: user_data
argument_reference.description: The user data to provide when launching the instance. This can be either plain text or base64 encoded text.

argument_reference.name: keypair
argument_reference.description: The name of the SSH key pair that will be used to access this instance.

argument_reference.name: expunge
argument_reference.description: This determines if the instance is expunged when it is destroyed (defaults false)

attributes_reference.name: display_name
attributes_reference.description: The display name of the instance.

import:
Instances can be imported; use <INSTANCE ID> as the import ID. For example:

terraform import cloudstack_instance.default 5cf69677-7e4b-4bf4-b868-f0b02bb72ee0When importing into a project you need to prefix the import ID with the project name:

terraform import cloudstack_instance.default my-project/5cf69677-7e4b-4bf4-b868-f0b02bb72ee0
